summary: Iter-136 Bas-Relief feature verified end-to-end. Backend: 11/11 API tests pass (POST /api/ai/generate/bas-relief happy path, custom params, image_url mode, all 5 rejection cases, auth gating, and quota-unchanged proof). Existing bas_relief_service unit tests (10) + iter-132.2 preview regression (14) still pass — no regression. Frontend: 31/31 UI checks pass — tab exists with amber accent + Circle icon, panel renders with 4 sliders + Invert checkbox, defaults 220/12/3, realtime slider updates, circular preview + total-thickness readout, amber CTA disabled without image, Loader2 spinner during gen, dialog auto-closes, disk imported to scene named 'bas-relief-*' at exactly 220×220×15mm, AND ai/usage 'used' count unchanged (0 → 0) confirming NO quota consumption.
critical_code_review_comments: bas_relief_service._build_disk_mesh() uses Python-level double for-loops to build faces (O(n²) with n=512 → 261K iterations per pass, 3 passes). Works but slow at max grid. Consider vectorising via numpy stride tricks if user reports latency > 3s.
bas_relief_service._is_edge is called per-pixel inside a list comprehension → another O(n²) pass. Same vectorisation opportunity.

AIGenerateDialog.jsx is 1182 lines — approaching the 700-line split threshold in code review guidelines. The bas-relief block (state + handler + panel + CTA) could be extracted into a dedicated BasReliefTab.jsx sub-component to reduce cognitive load.

Response headers stringify floats as '220.0', '12.0' — consumers expecting integers would need to `parseFloat`. Fine for the current single consumer (AIGenerateDialog) but worth noting for future callers.

The `X-Optimize-*` header namespace is reused from the mesh-optimize flow, which is semantically unrelated to bas-relief. Consider renaming to `X-BasRelief-*` for clarity, or documenting the shared prefix intent.
test_credentials: Session token 'st_prov132' bound to user_id 'user_prov132_2' (seeded via mongosh). Valid for 7 days from seed.
seed_data_creation: Seeded one user (user_prov132_2) + session (st_prov132) into MongoDB test_database. No other seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-136 backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iter136_bas_relief_api.py (11 tests). Note: the test fixture uses a TOP-TO-BOTTOM grayscale gradient PNG (not diagonal) so the brightest pixel column sits inside the disk mask — with a diagonal gradient the corner (brightest) is outside the circle and the peak Z falls short of base+max_relief by ~15%. iter-132.2 preview tests (already passing) at test_iter132_2_preview.py remain the canonical fal.ai smoke. To reproduce frontend flow: cookie session_token=st_prov132 → /workspace → LeftPanel 'AI' tab → 'Generate' button → 'Bas-Relief' tab → upload PNG → Generate.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Two tightening notes for the main agent: (a) initially my test image was a diagonal gradient which put the brightest pixel outside the circular mask, making the STL Z-extent measure ~13.2mm instead of the expected 15mm (base + max_relief). Fixed by switching to a vertical gradient. This is not a bug in the pipeline — the pipeline correctly reports `X-Optimize-Total-Height-Mm=15.0` and the mesh does reach 15mm only if the brightest pixel is inside the disk. (b) /api/ai/usage returns fields {used, cap, remaining, month, ...} — NOT `count`. Any future testing agent grepping for `.count` on the usage payload will get None; use `used` for quota-delta assertions.
